Test Case: Verify AuthService correctly interacts with CustomerRepository during registration
Test Steps:
1. Call AuthService.register() with valid RegisterRequest
2. Verify CustomerRepository.save() is called
3. Query CustomerRepository.findByEmail()
4. Verify customer record exists with correct data
Test Data: RegisterRequest: {name="Test User", email="test@example.com", telephone="+852 1234 5678", password="password123", customerType=PERSONAL}
Expected Results:
Customer saved to database
Customer.emailVerified = false
Customer.passwordHash is encrypted (not plain text)
Customer record retrievable by email

Test Case: Verify AuthService creates EmailVerification record during registration
Test Steps:
1. Call AuthService.register() with valid data
2. Verify EmailVerificationRepository.save() is called
3. Query EmailVerificationRepository by customer ID
4. Verify OTP record exists with correct expiration
Expected Results:
EmailVerification record created
otpCode is 6-digit string
expiresAt = current time + 10 minutes
verified = false

Test Case: Verify AuthService triggers email sending during registration
Pre-Conditions: Email service is configured and accessible
Test Steps:
1. Call AuthService.register() with valid data
2. Verify EmailService.sendVerificationEmailOrThrow() is called
3. Check email log or mock email service
4. Verify email contains OTP code
Expected Results:
EmailService.sendVerificationEmailOrThrow() invoked with correct email and OTP
Email sent successfully or logged
Email content includes 6-digit OTP

Test Case: Verify AuthService uses JwtUtil to generate tokens after verification
Test Steps:
1. Call AuthService.verifyEmail() with valid OTP
2. Verify JwtUtil.generateToken() is called with user email
3. Verify returned AuthResponse contains valid JWT token
4. Verify token can be validated by JwtUtil.validateToken()
Expected Results:
JwtUtil.generateToken() called with email
AuthResponse.token is non-empty JWT string
Token contains email in subject claim
Token expiration is set to 24 hours from now
Post-Condition: Valid JWT token generated and returned

Test Case: Verify AuthService uses PasswordEncoder to hash passwords
Test Steps:
1. Call AuthService.register() with plain text password
2. Verify PasswordEncoder.encode() is called
3. Query customer from database
4. Verify passwordHash is not equal to plain text password
5. Verify PasswordEncoder.matches() returns true for original password
Expected Results:
PasswordEncoder.encode() invoked
Customer.passwordHash != "mypassword123"
PasswordEncoder.matches("mypassword123", customer.passwordHash) = true

User A registration status = CANCEL
User B registration status = SUCCESS

Test Case: Verify waitlist promotion follows FIFO order
Pre-Conditions: Multiple WAIT registrations exist for same seminar
Test Steps:
1. Create 3 WAIT registrations at different times
2. Call RegistrationService.cancel() for SUCCESS registration
3. Verify RegistrationRepository.findBySeminarIdAndStatusOrderByCreatedAtAsc() is called
4. Verify earliest WAIT registration is promoted first
Test Data:
User A: WAIT created at T1
User B: WAIT created at T2
User C: WAIT created at T3
User D: SUCCESS (to be cancelled)
Expected Results:
RegistrationRepository query returns WAIT registrations ordered by createdAt ASC
User A (earliest) promoted to SUCCESS first
If seats remain, User B promoted next
Promotion emails sent in FIFO order

Test Case: Verify RegistrationService handles exact capacity correctly
Test Steps:
1. Call RegistrationService.register() with seatsBooked=2
2. Verify seat calculation: 8 + 2 = 10 (exactly at capacity)
3. Verify registration status = SUCCESS
4. Call RegistrationService.register() again with seatsBooked=1
5. Verify second registration status = WAIT
Expected Results:
First registration: currentBookedSeats=8, 8+2=10 <= 10, status=SUCCESS
Second registration: currentBookedSeats=10, 10+1=11 > 10, status=WAIT
